user_query:
I'm a Ph.D. candidate working on sentiment analysis and I want a small, reproducible web-sourced corpus of official corporate press releases to pilot a collaboration workflow. Please design and implement a minimal, scriptable architecture that discovers, downloads, and processes 2020 press releases from the official domains of Apple, Microsoft, and Google, then produces a labeled JSONL dataset and a concise report.

Use the provided inputs at input/config/targets.yaml (targets, domains, date range, labeling heuristics), input/schema.json (output field schema), and input/stopwords.txt (for summary term statistics). The pipeline must rely on a general-purpose web search engine to discover pages (no hardcoded URLs) and then download and extract the main article text from the discovered pages. Constrain discovery to the allowed domains and hints in the config. Save all artifacts under the workspace with the following required outputs and constraints:

1) Discovery and retrieval
- Use a general-purpose web search engine to construct queries from input/config/targets.yaml (site filters and hints) and collect at least 5 unique press releases per org (apple, microsoft, google) within 2020.
- Save raw HTML for each selected page to data/raw/<org>/<slug>.html where <org> ∈ {apple, microsoft, google} and <slug> is a filesystem-safe identifier derived from the title or URL.
- Create a machine-readable search log at data/metadata/search_log.jsonl where each line is a JSON object with at least: {"org", "query", "url"}. Record the exact query strings used and every chosen URL.
- Only download pages from the domains listed for each org in the config, and only if they appear to be press releases/newsroom posts dated in 2020. Skip duplicates by URL.

2) Extraction and processing
- Extract title, published_date (as ISO YYYY-MM-DD if available), and main text from each page. Drop pages with fewer than 500 characters of extracted text, as specified by the config.
- Produce data/processed/press_releases.jsonl that conforms to the field schema in input/schema.json. Every record must include: id, org, url, title, published_date (ISO string), text, char_count, word_count, label, and source_search_query. Compute char_count and word_count from the extracted text. Determine label using the keyword-based rules in the config (e.g., "announcement", "acquisition", "partnership"; if none match, set label to "other"). The number of JSONL records should be ≥ the number of raw HTML files that passed extraction/filters.

3) Reporting and documentation
- Write reports/summary.md with these sections:
  - Data coverage: counts per org for discovered, downloaded, successfully parsed, and filtered-out pages.
  - Label distribution: counts per label.
  - Top 20 terms (lowercased, non-stopwords based on input/stopwords.txt) across the corpus; show each term with its frequency.
  - Reproducibility: the exact command to run the pipeline and a brief note on how queries were constructed from the config.
- Provide docs/architecture.md describing the high-level architecture (discovery, fetching, parsing, labeling, data outputs) and key design choices.

4) Orchestration and reproducibility
- Include a runnable entrypoint script scripts/run.sh that executes the end-to-end pipeline (discovery → download → extract → process → report) and writes a timestamped log at logs/run.log. The pipeline must run from the project root without interactive input, using only the provided inputs and public web pages. Do not require API keys or paid services.

Constraints and verification notes
- Discovery must be performed via search engine queries; do not hardcode direct URLs. Use the domain and hint information in input/config/targets.yaml to generate site-filtered queries, and save the query strings in data/metadata/search_log.jsonl.
- All output paths listed above must be created.
- Enforce the date range (2020-01-01 to 2020-12-31) and domain constraints from the config.
- Ensure data/processed/press_releases.jsonl conforms to input/schema.json. The counts in reports/summary.md must be consistent with the files produced under data/raw and data/processed.

Deliverables to verify:
- data/raw/<org>/*.html (≥5 per org, 2020, under allowed domains)
- data/metadata/search_log.jsonl (query and URL provenance)
- data/processed/press_releases.jsonl (schema-compliant records)
- reports/summary.md (required sections)
- docs/architecture.md (architecture overview)
- scripts/run.sh (runnable entrypoint)
- logs/run.log (execution log)

Please proceed with this architecture and produce the above artifacts. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
